summary:
With an overall score of 89/100, Eightcap Challenges is one of the highest-scoring prop firms we've reviewed. The challenge website is operated by Eightcap International Ltd in Seychelles, and while the environment is designed to mirror live conditions, all activities are simulated and do not create a brokerage client relationship.

The Day Trader, One Phase and Two Phase challenges cover both structured multi-day evaluations and fast timed sessions, with RAW account spreads starting from 0.0 pips or points and a $5 per lot commission on all instruments except Cryptocurrency ($0). Both One Phase and Two Phase challenges range from $5,000 to $200,000, with the standard profit split at 80% (up to 90% only with the paid add-on). We think this will suit serious forex traders who want a trading environment that feels familiar and efficient.
pros: RAW spreads from 0.0 pips with $5/lot commission
MT4, MT5, and TradeLocker platform access
Unique Day Trader challenge format (1-8 hour sessions)
Three distinct challenge types for different trading styles
Clear, transparent rules across all account types
cons: One Phase profit cap of 5% on all payouts
Two Phase profit cap of 10% on all payouts
News trading restricted around high-impact events
Higher fees on larger account sizes
14-day wait before first payout on standard challenges
bestFor: Serious forex traders who want broker-level infrastructure, consistent pricing with multiple challenge formats including unique short-session Day Trader challenges
notIdealFor: Traders wanting traditional scaling plans, unlimited profit withdrawal on standard challenges from day one, or those needing instant payouts on standard accounts

verdictBlocks.heading: Our Final Verdict On Eightcap Challenges
verdictBlocks.body:
Eightcap Challenges earns a solid overall score of 89/100. The challenge website is operated by Eightcap International Ltd in Seychelles, and while the environment is designed to mirror live conditions, all activities are simulated and do not create a brokerage client relationship.

The challenge formats are balanced and achievable. The Day Trader model offers a genuinely alternative way to prop trade, and MT4, MT5 and TradeLocker support gives you access to a proper technical environment. Costs are competitive starting from $59, RAW spreads start from 0.0 pips, and the trading rules are clear and transparent.

The main limitations are the profit caps on payouts (5% for One Phase, 10% for Two Phase), no traditional scaling plan, and the 14-day wait before the first payout on standard challenges. The overall package feels more stable and predictable than many independent prop firms because everything is built on top of Eightcap's infrastructure.

faq.q: What challenge types does Eightcap Challenges offer?
faq.a: Eightcap Challenges offers three evaluation types: Day Trader, One Phase, and Two Phase. One Phase and Two Phase account sizes range from $5,000 to $200,000. Day Trader uses a fixed $10,000 balance with stakes from $5 to $500. All challenges operate in a simulated environment.

faq.q: How long do Eightcap Challenges payouts take?
faq.a: Payouts are typically processed within 2 business days and only on business days. The first payout for One Phase and Two Phase accounts is available after 14 days (or 7 days with the Earlier Payout add-on), subject to minimum trading day requirements. Minimum payout is $100 after the profit split for standard challenges. Crypto withdrawals incur a 1% fee and bank wire withdrawals incur a flat $50 fee.

faq.q: What are the profit caps on Eightcap Challenges?
faq.a: One Phase profit cap is 5% of the initial balance and applies to all approved payouts. Two Phase profit cap is 10% of the initial balance for all approved payouts. Accounts created before February 11 are not subject to a profit cap for the Two Phase challenge. Only fully earned profit can be withdrawn — partial payouts are not supported.

faq.q: Is there an Eightcap Challenges demo account for challenges?
faq.a: No, Eightcap Challenges does not offer a demo version of its prop challenges. The challenge environment uses simulated conditions, but you can't trial the evaluation rules or test pass conditions without buying a challenge first. If you want to practise, you can use a free MT4 or MT5 demo, but it won't replicate challenge drawdowns or targets.
